# Create an alias suffix for a scope

Creating aliases at the org and project scope level allows scope administrators to manage their own aliases, seeing only the resources that belong to their scopes.

When you create an alias in an org or project scope, you must also create a suffix for the scope.
Once you create the suffix, Boundary appends the suffix to any aliases you create in that scope.
You can reuse alias values across org and project scopes because the suffix makes them globally unique values.

Org suffixes must be globally unique.
Project suffixes must be unique within the org the project resides in.

## Permissions

@include '/suffixes/permissions.mdx'

## Create the suffix

Complete the following steps to create a suffix for an alias:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="UI" group="ui">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org or project scope you want to create a suffix for.
1. Click **Create Alias suffix**.
1. In the **Alias suffix** field, type a unique value for the suffix. Boundary will append this suffix to any aliases you create within the org or project scope.
1. Click **Save**.

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to create an alias suffix:

   ```shell-session
   $ boundary scopes set-alias-suffix \
       -id o_1234567890 \
       -alias-suffix org1
   ```

   You must provide the the following attributes when you create a suffix:

   - `-id=<string>` - Scope in which to create the suffix.
   You can also specify the scope using the BOUNDARY_SCOPE_ID environment variable.
   - `-alias-suffix=<string>` - Specifies the string that you want to use as the suffix to append to aliases in the designated scope.
   The `alias-suffix` can be a hostname or a DNS-like string.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Next steps

Once you have created a suffix for an org or project scope, you can [Create a target alias](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration/create-target-alias) for any targets that reside in that scope.

## More information

For more information about aliases, review the [Overview of aliases](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration).
